title: Unlimited message size in readMessage allows memory exhaustion DoS
description: The readMessage function reads a 4-byte message length from the network and allocates a buffer of that size via readExactly(size: Int(messageLength)). Since messageLength is a UInt32, the maximum value is 4,294,967,295 (4 GB). A malicious client can send a length prefix of 0xFFFFFFFF, causing the server to attempt allocating 4 GB of memory per connection. With multiple concurrent connections, this trivially causes an out-of-memory server crash. The readExactly function at line 688 calls buffer.reserveCapacity(size) with the untrusted size value.
recommendation: Add a maximum message size constant (e.g., 16 MB or 64 MB depending on expected maximum message size for file transfers) and reject messages that exceed it: 'guard messageLength <= MAX_MESSAGE_SIZE else { throw P7SocketError.readFailed("Message too large") }'.
patch_note: Duplicate of FINDING_P_002/P_001 — fixed in same commit
